name: Kimi K2 Thinking
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 384 experts (8 active + 1 shared/token), native INT4 quantization-aware training
note: Open-weight 1T MoE reasoning agent with native INT4 weights and 256K context, chaining 200-300 sequential tool calls.
tpsNote: Does not fit on a single RTX 4090 (24GB); native INT4 packs ~500-600GB combined RAM/VRAM, community reports only ~5-10 tok/s with heavy CPU/SSD offload

results.notes: General Tasks table, MMLU-Pro score on official model card
General Tasks table, MMLU-Redux score on official model card
LiveCodeBench V6, no tools, from Coding Tasks table on official model card
Terminal-Bench score evaluated with the model's simulated-JSON tool-calling harness (per model card's own footnote), from Coding Tasks table

tau2-Bench Telecom (agentic tool-use, customer-service-agent eval), independently measured by Artificial Analysis at launch: 'K2 Thinking achieving 93% in τ²-Bench Telecom ... the highest score we have independently measured,' driving its #2 position (behind only GPT-5) in AA's Agentic Index. Distinct from and not previously tracked alongside the corpus's existing 9 self-reported HF-model-card results (which cover HLE, SWE-bench, AIME25, GPQA, BrowseComp, MMLU-Pro, MMLU-Redux, LiveCodeBench, Terminal-Bench but no tau-bench variant).

IFBench (instruction-following), independently measured by Artificial Analysis; read directly from AA's live ranked bar chart for Kimi K2 Thinking (68%, ranked 15th of 23 models shown). Cross-confirmed by a third-party AA-data mirror (graysoft.dev) showing 68.1%. Not self-reported by Moonshot AI and not previously present in the corpus for this model.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real model by directly fetching huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2-Thinking: verbatim matched 1T total/32B active/384 experts (8 active)/256K context/Modified MIT License and all 5 benchmark scores (HLE-with-tools 44.9, SWE-bench 71.3, AIME25 99.1, GPQA 84.5, BrowseComp 60.2). Release date Nov 6, 2025 corroborated by CNBC and NIST CAISI evaluation coverage. Pricing $0.60/$2.50 per M tokens confirmed via OpenRouter listing.

2026-07-16: Re-checked the four missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval, mmmu) specifically for the Kimi K2 Thinking checkpoint (Nov 2025). Fetched the official HF model card directly: its General/Reasoning/Coding tables report MMLU-Pro (84.6) and MMLU-Redux (94.4) but no plain MMLU; AIME25 (up to 100.0) but no MATH-500; LiveCodeBench V6 (83.1) but no HumanEval; MMMU does not appear at all. Cross-checked Artificial Analysis, Epoch AI, and Vals.ai model pages for this model -- none show scores for any of the four. Sibling model Kimi K2 (base, July 2025) did publish MMLU=89.5 and MATH-500=97.4, confirming Moonshot deliberately superseded both with MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux and AIME25 for the Thinking release rather than never testing them; HumanEval has never been reported for any model in the Kimi K2 family (LiveCodeBench is their standing coding benchmark). MMMU is additionally inapplicable on capability grounds: the model's own spec lists modalities:["text"] and capabilities.vision:false (no image input), and Moonshot's line only gained native multimodal support with the later Kimi K2.5 release. All four treated as genuine, expected absences -- no scores added.

2026-07-17 (second-pass, non-obvious sources): Chased mmlu/math/humaneval via 6 source types the first pass had not touched. (1) Stanford HELM: checked crfm.stanford.edu/helm leaderboard pages directly (JS-rendered, no extractable table via fetch) and, more conclusively, the stanford-crfm/helm GitHub repo's CHANGELOG.md and Releases -- HELM has only ever added 'Kimi K2' (v0.5.7, 2025-07-31) and 'Kimi K2 Instruct 0905' (v0.5.12, 2026-01-27) to its supported-model roster; 'Kimi K2 Thinking' has never been integrated into HELM, so no HELM MMLU/MATH/HumanEval run exists for this checkpoint at all -- a definitive absence, not a missed lookup. (2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar: fetched the full HTML of the Kimi K2.5 technical report (arXiv 2602.02276v1), the one paper in the family that post-dates and directly compares against K2 Thinking. Its Table 5 ('Performance and token efficiency of some reasoning models') explicitly lists Kimi K2 Thinking as a baseline with AIME2025=94.5, HMMT-Feb2025=89.4, HMMT-Nov2025=89.2, IMO-AnswerBench=78.6, LiveCodeBench=82.6, GPQA-Diamond=84.5, HLE-Text=23.9 -- but pointedly omits MMLU, MMLU-Pro, MATH, MATH-500, and HumanEval for every model in that table, K2 Thinking included. The original Kimi K2 (base) paper (arXiv 2507.20534) reports MMLU/MATH-500 only for the earlier non-thinking checkpoint, already accounted for in the prior verificationNotes entry. (3) GitHub eval-harness logs: found a public gist (gist.github.com/abhishekmishragithub, 'kimi-k2-thinking-eval-benchamark') showing an lm-evaluation-harness integration run against Kimi-K2-Thinking via vLLM, but it covers only GSM8K-CoT and HellaSwag on 20-example slices and explicitly self-labels these as smoke tests 'not to be treated as final metrics' -- no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval task was run. A vLLM CI job ('LM Eval coverage for Kimi K2 Thinking and MiniMax M2.7') is an infra-correctness check, not a published benchmark score. (4) Other leaderboards: OpenRouter's dedicated /benchmarks subpage for the model renders scores as a JS chart with no extractable backing text; Scale AI SEAL only has a Seal-0 (real-world web retrieval) score for this model, not MMLU/MATH/HumanEval; LiveBench.ai's page is likewise JS-rendered with no scrapeable table; no PapersWithCode leaderboard entry for this specific checkpoint was found. (5) Non-English sources: multiple Chinese community write-ups (Zhihu) surfaced in search but returned HTTP 403 on fetch; search-snippet coverage of Chinese/Japanese/Korean discussion surfaced only restatements of the already-known MMLU-Pro figure (~84.6-84.8), no new plain-MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval number. (6) Sibling models: cross-checked both the Kimi K2 base paper and the Kimi K2.5 report's comparison tables (see #2) -- neither table includes MMLU/MATH/HumanEval for the Thinking checkpoint specifically. One low-quality aggregator (automatio.ai) claimed MMLU=89.4, MATH=94.1, HumanEval=99 for this model, but was rejected as unreliable: the same page also attributes MMMU=65.8, MMMU-Pro=62.4, MathVista=36.8, ChartQA=86.2, and DocVQA=94.5 to Kimi K2 Thinking -- vision benchmarks that are impossible for this model, which is verified text-only (modalities:[\"text\"], capabilities.vision:false), indicating the aggregator conflated its data with the vision-capable sibling Kimi K2.5; none of its numbers were treated as credible. Conclusion: mmlu, math, and humaneval remain genuine, well-attested absences for the Kimi K2 Thinking checkpoint specifically -- Moonshot has consistently substituted MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux, AIME25, and LiveCodeBench in its place across the entire K2 family's public materials, and no independent academic, leaderboard, or eval-harness source across 6 checked source types has run or published legacy MMLU/MATH/HumanEval numbers for it. No new results added this round.
